title: Human Life
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ What mortal, when he saw,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Lifeן¿½s voyage done, his heavenly Friend,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Could ever yet dare tell him fearlessly:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½I have kept uninfringן¿½d my natureן¿½s law;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The inly-written chart thou gayest me
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To guide me, I have steerן¿½d by to the endן¿½?

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Ah! let us make no claim
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On lifeן¿½s incognizable sea
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ To too exact a steering of our way!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Let us not fret and fear to miss our aim
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ If some fair coast has lured us to make stay,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Or some friend hailן¿½d us to keep company !

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Aye, we would each fain drive
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ At random, and not steer by rule!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Weakness! and worse, weakness bestowן¿½d in vain!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Winds from our side the unsuiting consort rive,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We rush by coasts where we had lief remain;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Man cannot, though he would, live chanceן¿½s fool.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ No! as the foaming swathe
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of torn-up water, on the main,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Falls heavily away with long-drawn roar
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On either side the black deep-furrowן¿½d path
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Cut by an onward-labouring vesselן¿½s prore,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And never touches the ship-side again;

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Even so we leave behind,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As, charterן¿½d by some unknown Powers,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ We stem across the sea of life by night,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The joys which were not for our use designן¿½d,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The friends to whom we had no natural right,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The homes that were not destined to be ours.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Matthew Arnold represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Matthew Arnold's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
